orgs.States of Operation: AZ, FL, GA, MI, MT, NC, NV, OH, PA, WI
orgs.Evidence Summary: MVP 2024 field plan: $3.2M — significant investment signal. Hub-and-spoke model across 10 battleground states.

orgs.Primary Focus: Year-round state and local election administration advocacy and voter access expansion
orgs.States of Operation: AZ, FL, GA, MI, NV, OH, PA, WI
orgs.Evidence Summary: Expanded access for 13M+ voters in 2024 cycle. Multiple independent sources. Fills gap other orgs miss.

orgs.Primary Focus: National coordination hub for the progressive ecosystem — aligns state tables, community orgs, and national groups across 20+ states
orgs.States of Operation: AZ, GA, MI, MN, NC, NH, NV, OH, PA, WI (20+ states)
orgs.Evidence Summary: Invested $200M in 2024 cycle, delivering 56M+ door knocks across the country. Harris ran ahead of national vote share in every AV battleground state despite overall loss. 400+ partner organizations. 2026 State Summit April 20-23 signals active 2026 engagement.

orgs.Primary Focus: Connects blue-state volunteers and donors to competitive state legislative races
orgs.States of Operation: AZ, GA, MI, MN, NC, NH, NV, PA, WI
orgs.Evidence Summary: MVP 2024 field plan: $1.8M. State leg focus is distinctive and complements federal race orgs.

orgs.Primary Focus: Deploys out-of-state volunteers directly into locally-rooted grassroots orgs in battleground states
orgs.States of Operation: AZ, GA, PA, FL, NC, WI, NV (and others)
orgs.Evidence Summary: MVP describes STV as 'the best at deploying volunteers to phone bank and canvas in person.' Distinctive model routes volunteers to local orgs.

orgs.Primary Focus: Tipped/subminimum wage workers advocacy + voter mobilization
orgs.Evidence Summary: MVP 2024 field plan: $3M. Issue-based organizing with strong electoral tie-in.

orgs.Primary Focus: Climate-first voter mobilization for Americans 60+ in battleground states
orgs.States of Operation: AZ, PA, MI, NH, WI, GA
orgs.Evidence Summary: Founded by Bill McKibben. 100,000+ volunteers self-reported. Distinctive senior climate niche with no direct competitor. Multiple independent sources.

orgs.Primary Focus: CA-based farmworker/Latino civic org — deep canvass
orgs.Evidence Summary: STV 2026 current partner. 20+ year track record. CA House seats on Swing Left target map.

orgs.Primary Focus: Largest immigrant youth-led organization in the country — civic engagement, voter mobilization, and immigration advocacy
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: Immigrant voters, Youth voters, Latino voters
orgs.Evidence Summary: Largest immigrant youth-led org in the country with 100,000+ members. Immigration is a top-3 motivating issue for progressive donors in 2026. Deep trust in immigrant communities that other orgs cannot replicate.

orgs.Primary Focus: Southern Black voter mobilization
orgs.Evidence Summary: ChatGPT flagged as major omission. Key GA ecosystem piece post-NGP.

orgs.Primary Focus: Domestic workers org — voter mobilization in GA/NC/AZ/NV
orgs.Evidence Summary: MVP 2024 field plan: $3.2M + STV 2020 national partner — two strong independent signals.

orgs.Primary Focus: AAPI voter registration, GOTV, multilingual outreach in Georgia
orgs.Evidence Summary: Part of national Advancing Justice network. AAPI voters D at 57-59% in GA in 2020/2022. Multiple independent sources.

orgs.Primary Focus: Latino civic engagement, voter registration, and leadership development in Georgia
orgs.Evidence Summary: 20+ year track record in GA. Now primary Latino civic engagement org after NGP closure — elevated importance for 2026.

orgs.Primary Focus: Statewide voter registration, GOTV, coalition coordination across Georgia
orgs.Evidence Summary: State Voices affiliate now serving as primary GA civic infrastructure after NGP closure Oct 2025. Urgency elevated by ecosystem gap.

orgs.Primary Focus: Black and Brown working-class voter mobilization in Detroit/Michigan
orgs.Evidence Summary: Featured on MVP about page — strong practitioner endorsement. Year-round membership canvass model is high-credibility.

orgs.Primary Focus: Statewide voter registration, GOTV, coalition coordination across Michigan
orgs.Evidence Summary: State Voices affiliate — vetted network with consistent multi-cycle track record.

orgs.Primary Focus: Pro-choice Democratic women candidates — one of the largest donor networks in Dem politics
orgs.Evidence Summary: One of largest donor networks in Dem politics. 40+ year track record.

orgs.Primary Focus: LGBTQ+ civil rights advocacy, candidate endorsements, and electoral program nationally
orgs.Evidence Summary: 3.6M members. 45+ year track record. 75M+ Equality Voter outreach planned for 2026.

orgs.Primary Focus: One of largest environmental electoral spenders
orgs.Evidence Summary: One of largest environmental electoral spenders. 55+ year track record. Super PAC arm deploys tens of millions.

orgs.Primary Focus: 30M+ member email list — digital + field mobilizer
orgs.Evidence Summary: 30M+ member list (widely cited). Battleground Alliance founding partner. 25+ year track record.

orgs.Primary Focus: Electoral arm of Planned Parenthood — reproductive rights as electoral mobilizer
orgs.Evidence Summary: Battleground Alliance founding partner. Reproductive rights is highly relevant post-Dobbs. Major institutional credibility.

orgs.Primary Focus: Clearinghouse directing donor funds to local grassroots orgs in battleground states nationally
orgs.Evidence Summary: Raised $6.2M in 2025. Rigorous vetting process. High-leverage model. Multiple practitioner endorsements. 2026 focus: Battleground Alliance for House flip.

orgs.Org Name: SURJ — Showing Up for Racial Justice
orgs.Primary Focus: Mobilizes white progressive volunteers into phone banking and voter contact programs
orgs.Evidence Summary: MVP highlights their phone bank as filling a distinctive gap. Seed the Vote national partner. 200+ local chapters.

orgs.Primary Focus: National network of 30+ state/local member orgs running deep-canvassing, phone banks, text banking
orgs.Evidence Summary: MVP calls them 'extremely adept at onboarding and absorbing new volunteers.' Deep canvassing model has evidence base. Down Home NC is an affiliate.

orgs.Primary Focus: Volunteer-powered grassroots donor and canvassing operation targeting competitive House districts
orgs.Evidence Summary: 1M+ grassroots members; raised $25M+ in 2024. 2026: Ground Truth deep-listening canvass in 33 target districts. Does NOT involve itself in primaries.

orgs.Primary Focus: Official Democratic Party committee for winning and defending state legislative majorities nationally
orgs.Evidence Summary: $50M budget for 2026 — largest ever. 42-chamber target map. Democrats overperformed 2025 targets by 4.5 points on average. State leg control shapes redistricting in 2030 and is where progressive policy gets made.

orgs.Primary Focus: National grassroots network for civic engagement, GOTV, and candidate endorsements
orgs.Evidence Summary: Historically general-election focused with strong track record. 2026 primary engagement is new and expanded — monitor.

orgs.Donations Tax Deductible: Unconfirmed — 501(c)(4); possible c3 affiliate; verify before advising donors
orgs.Primary Focus: Volunteer-driven handwritten letters to low-propensity voters; RCT-tested turnout program; repositioning toward new tactic development for 2026
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: GOTV; remote volunteering; evidence-based voter contact; low-propensity voters; historically marginalized communities
orgs.States of Operation: National (all states; 2026 priority: PA new voter program, VA redistricting referendum, competitive House districts)
orgs.Evidence Summary: SPLIT RATING. Evidence quality: Tier 1 — 25+ RCTs conducted, 20 of 25 showing positive turnout effects; among the strongest publicly available evidence bases in the field (distinct from Analyst Institute-backed orgs whose results are private). Projected 2026 effectiveness: Tier 2 — Vote Forward itself acknowledges declining effectiveness in high-salience elections including 2024 due to oversaturation. For 2026 competitive House races, channel substitution (simultaneous texts/mail/ads/canvassing), volunteer quality variance, and targeting efficiency concerns all moderate expected impact. Strongest in low-salience, low-information environments.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: One of the most rigorously tested remote volunteer programs in the progressive ecosystem — but donors should understand that letter-writing effectiveness has declined in high-salience races, and the 2026 investment case depends on successful new tactic development.
orgs.Key Risks & Flags: 1) TACTIC CONTEXT-DEPENDENCY: Effectiveness Tier 1 historically but Tier 2 projected for 2026 high-salience competitive House races. 2) CHANNEL SUBSTITUTION: In contested districts, marginal letter adds limited incremental signal when competing with texts, mail, ads, canvassing simultaneously. 3) VOLUNTEER QUALITY VARIANCE: 285K distributed volunteers introduce message fidelity variation; RCT conditions may not generalize to real-world deployment. 4) TRANSITION RISK: Repositioning to innovation model introduces 2026 execution uncertainty. 5) IRS STATUS FLAG (URGENT): ProPublica warning must be verified before finalizing recommendation. 6) REVENUE VOLATILITY: Heavy election-cycle dependence; 2025 likely sharp trough. 7) NOT TAX DEDUCTIBLE: 501(c)(4); c3 pathway unconfirmed. 8) INCOMPLETE DILIGENCE: Staff count, program ratio, audit, Candid seal not confirmed.

orgs.Primary Focus: Latino civic engagement, voter registration, and voter education (digital-first, youth focus)
orgs.Evidence Summary: 2M+ voters registered since founding — consistent across sources. 20-year track record. Strong digital/campus presence.

orgs.Primary Focus: Civil rights litigation, voting rights defense, and Black voter civic engagement nationally
orgs.States of Operation: National (focus: AL, FL, GA, LA, MS, NC, SC, TX)
orgs.Evidence Summary: Founded 1940 by Thurgood Marshall. 85-year track record. LDF litigation directly protects votes cast by state table partners.

orgs.Primary Focus: AFL-CIO community affiliate canvassing non-union working-class voters door-to-door on kitchen table issues; 5M+ members
orgs.States of Operation: National (strong: OH, PA, MI, WI, MN, NC, GA)
orgs.Evidence Summary: 5M+ members (non-union workers). Year-round door-to-door canvassing in working-class neighborhoods on kitchen table issues. AFL-CIO provides $12M+ in support per cycle. Largest non-employer-based workers group in the country. Distinct from unions — donors CAN support Working America even if dropping unions.

orgs.Primary Focus: Black voter mobilization and GOTV in battleground states
orgs.States of Operation: NC, GA, MI, PA, NV, WI
orgs.Evidence Summary: Founded by Adrianne Shropshire; incubated by SEIU. Year-round canvassing model. Does NOT run primary challenges.

orgs.Primary Focus: Major progressive coalition hub for NY field program
orgs.Evidence Summary: MVP named grantee. 40+ year track record. NY has multiple competitive House seats in 2026.

orgs.Primary Focus: Statewide civic engagement coordination, 50+ org coalition in NC
orgs.Evidence Summary: 50+ org coalition with multi-cycle track record. Multiple independent sources. NC is Tier 1 battleground for 2026.

orgs.Primary Focus: Rural and small-town multiracial organizing and voter engagement in NC
orgs.Evidence Summary: People's Action affiliate. Distinctive rural white working-class focus fills gap other orgs don't. Essential for Cooper Senate path.

orgs.Primary Focus: NC-based Latino/immigrant org
orgs.Evidence Summary: MVP named grantee. NC is critical 2026 battleground.

orgs.Primary Focus: NC community organizing
orgs.Evidence Summary: Featured on MVP about page alongside Detroit Action and LUCHA — strong practitioner endorsement.

orgs.Primary Focus: Electoral endorsements and fusion ballot line supporting progressive candidates nationally
orgs.States of Operation: NY, CT, NJ, PA, OR, MD, DC, WI, RI, CA, WV
orgs.Evidence Summary: 25+ year track record. 2026: endorsing primary challengers. Does NOT challenge Frontline members. Donor alert: may conflict with DCCC-aligned strategy.

orgs.Primary Focus: Latino organizing — NY/NJ/NV/PA presence
orgs.Evidence Summary: ChatGPT gap list + known practitioner reputation. Multi-cycle track record in NY/NJ.

orgs.Primary Focus: Statewide voter engagement and coalition coordination in Ohio
orgs.Evidence Summary: State Voices affiliate with multi-cycle track record.

orgs.Primary Focus: OH youth/student organizing
orgs.Evidence Summary: MVP 2026 youth briefing. OH-01 and OH-13 are MVP House targets.

orgs.Primary Focus: Statewide voter registration, GOTV, coalition coordination (50+ partner orgs)
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: Multiracial democracy; BIPOC voter engagement; voting rights; voter registration; civic infrastructure
orgs.Evidence Summary: Ecosystem-backed, directional — not causally validated at org level. Documented outputs: 122K voters registered, Vote by Mail legislative victory (multi-actor), 2025 PA Supreme Court retention wins (coalition). MVP Neighborhood Captains RCT with David Nickerson underway — results pending.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The statewide coordinating infrastructure for Pennsylvania's progressive civic ecosystem — a single grant simultaneously activates 50+ partner organizations across the most contested state of the 2026 cycle.
orgs.Key Risks & Flags: 1) Regrantor dilution: impact depends on partner quality; no visibility into how PA Voice enforces accountability across 50+ grantees. 2) Rapid revenue growth (3.8→8.6M) could indicate outpacing of internal capacity. 3) Revenue concentration unknown — Schedule B legally redacted.

orgs.Primary Focus: Organization name not confirmed
orgs.Evidence Summary: VERIFY WITH COLLEAGUE. Does not match any identifiable progressive electoral org. Likely confusion with Priorities USA or American Bridge 21st Century.

orgs.Primary Focus: Statewide voter registration, GOTV, power building for communities of color in VA
orgs.Evidence Summary: Multi-cycle track record in VA. Credited with helping flip VA legislature. Multiple independent sources.

orgs.Primary Focus: Small business owner organizing — trusted messenger strategy
orgs.States of Operation: WI, VA, NC, IA, MN, MI
orgs.Evidence Summary: Battleground Alliance partner. Distinctive non-traditional base fills gap other orgs miss.

orgs.Primary Focus: WI youth civic engagement
orgs.Evidence Summary: Featured on MVP about page. WI is top-tier Senate + House battleground.

orgs.Primary Focus: Latino and immigrant voter mobilization and civic engagement in Wisconsin
orgs.Evidence Summary: 30+ year track record. Political action arm active since 2004. 33,600 new eligible Latino voters in WI since 2020.

orgs.Primary Focus: Statewide voter engagement and coalition coordination in Wisconsin
orgs.Evidence Summary: State Voices affiliate. Multi-cycle track record. Critical for open Gov, WI-03, and two Toss-Up state legislative chambers.

orgs.Primary Focus: Organizes Black trade unionists and Black communities around voter registration, civic engagement, and labor-civil rights coalition building through 114 chapters in 36 states; serves as the AFL-CIO's primary outreach vehicle to Black communities.
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: Black voters, Labor, Voting rights, Civic engagement
orgs.States of Operation: National (114 chapters in 36 states; battleground concentration in OH, PA, MI, WI, NC, GA, NV)
orgs.Evidence Summary: AFL-CIO constituency group with 60-year track record; 114 chapters in 36 states; GuideStar confirms active civic engagement program across target states; FY2025 990 shows $3.07M revenue suggesting active 2026 cycle investment.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The 60-year-old bridge between the labor movement and Black civic engagement — 114 chapters in 36 states running voter registration and GOTV through a network no other org has built.
orgs.Key Risks & Flags: (1) Three consecutive years of net losses FY2022–FY2024, negative net assets in two of those years — balance sheet is structurally thin outside election cycle. (2) Revenue is heavily election-cycle-dependent; off-year capacity is limited. (3) 501(c)(4) status means donations not tax-deductible — tax-planning donors must give to APREF (ORG-103) instead. (4) Chapter quality varies significantly across 36 states — national brand does not guarantee local program quality. (5) Sharp drop in Brown compensation in FY2025 ($6,500 vs. ~$107K in FY2021) — flag for clarification; may reflect compensation through AFL-CIO or related entity.

orgs.Sub-type: Black labor civic engagement (tax-deductible giving vehicle for APRI)
orgs.Primary Focus: Serves as the 501(c)(3) tax-deductible giving vehicle for the APRI ecosystem; receives contributions and channels them to support APRI's civic engagement, voter registration, and GOTV programs through the national chapter network.
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: Black voters, Labor, Voting rights, Civic engagement
orgs.Evidence Summary: Companion 501(c)(3) to APRI (ORG-102); shares president, address, and mission; 990s consistently filed since 1980; board includes Eleanor Holmes Norton, Dr. William Julius Wilson, and Ernest Green — strong governance credibility. Donor-facing rationale is tax deductibility, not independent program capacity.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The tax-deductible giving vehicle for America's oldest Black labor-civil rights civic engagement network — the right entry point for donors who need a c3 to fund APRI's 114-chapter GOTV operation.
orgs.Key Risks & Flags: (1) APREF has no independent program capacity — it is a conduit for tax-deductible giving to APRI; donors are effectively funding APRI through this vehicle. (2) Revenue is small and election-cycle-dependent; FY2025 $144K suggests off-cycle thinning. (3) All risks flagged in ORG-102 (APRI) apply here — the two entities are operationally inseparable. (4) Donors should understand they are not funding a standalone c3 organization with its own staff and programs.

orgs.Org Name: Asian American Community Fund of Pennsylvania (AACFPA)
orgs.ProPublica URL: Search ProPublica -- no listing found in this session
orgs.Primary Focus: Supports local Asian American associations and organizations across Pennsylvania; runs civic engagement, voter registration, and GOTV programs focused on AAPI communities in Philadelphia, collar counties, and Bucks County.
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: AAPI voters, Civic engagement, Voting rights
orgs.Evidence Summary: Self-reported program data on website; org notes community partners doubled Asian American vote share in Pennsylvania and that PA AAPI population doubled since 2000 with highest vote-by-mail sign-up rate of any racial group in the Commonwealth. No independent third-party evaluation found.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The AAPI civic infrastructure for a Pennsylvania electorate where Asian Americans are the fastest-growing demographic -- doubled since 2000 -- and delivered the highest vote-by-mail sign-up rate of any racial group in the state.
orgs.Key Risks & Flags: (1) No ProPublica listing -- EIN, tax status, and financials entirely unverified. (2) May be a very new or very small org operating below 990 filing threshold. (3) No independent practitioner endorsement (MVP, Way to Win, etc.) found. (4) Entity structure unclear -- could be fiscally sponsored or newly incorporated.

orgs.Primary Focus: Organizes Latino and immigrant communities in Philadelphia, the Lehigh Valley, and Bucks County through leadership development, civic engagement, voter registration, and direct action; chapter of Make the Road States national network.
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: Latino voters, Immigration, Civic engagement, Voting rights
orgs.Evidence Summary: MVP partner confirmed for 2025-2026 PA Supreme Court retention and 2026 cycle per MVP Pennsylvania 2025 Plan page; part of coordinated WFP-led coalition of 17 PA groups. Make the Road network has multi-state track record in NY, NJ, NV, PA.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The Latino organizing infrastructure in the Lehigh Valley and Bucks County -- the two geographies most decisive in PA-07 -- with MVP backing and a proven multi-state network behind it.
orgs.Key Risks & Flags: (1) 501(c)(4) -- donations not tax-deductible. (2) Pennsylvania chapter financials subsumed under national Make the Road States entity -- no standalone PA 990. (3) Immigration-enforcement-resistance stance may create competitive-district friction in PA-07 (as flagged in ORG-049 national entry). (4) Possible duplication with ORG-049 (Make the Road Action) already in sheet -- this row is PA-specific geography context.

orgs.Sub-type: Black community civic engagement and cross-racial organizing
orgs.Primary Focus: Organizes Black communities and cross-racial coalitions in Philadelphia, Harrisburg, and Pittsburgh through political education, activist leadership development, media campaigns, voter mobilization, and issue advocacy; subordinate org under Organize Pennsylvania.
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: Black voters, Civic engagement, Economic justice, Voting rights
orgs.Evidence Summary: MVP partner confirmed for 2025-2026 PA cycle per MVP Pennsylvania 2025 Plan; FY2023 revenue $2.75M and assets $1.3M confirm meaningful organizational scale; received $716,895 grant from Voter Registration Project in 2023.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The cross-racial organizing infrastructure connecting Philadelphia, Pittsburgh, and Harrisburg -- MVP-backed, $2.75M in revenue, and part of the coordinated PA progressive coalition ahead of three toss-up House races.
orgs.Key Risks & Flags: (1) Founded 2022 -- relatively young org with limited multi-cycle track record. (2) No full-time employees listed on IRS filing -- compensation and staffing structure needs clarification. (3) Subordinate to Organize Pennsylvania -- relationship and governance implications warrant Phase 3 review. (4) $0 executive compensation on 990 is unusual at this revenue scale.

orgs.Primary Focus: Runs deep multiracial field organizing in small cities, towns, and rural areas across Pennsylvania -- particularly Lehigh Valley, Lancaster, Scranton, Reading, and York -- using Race Class Narrative framework to reach poor white voters alongside communities of color.
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: Civic engagement, Multi-issue, Voting rights, Economic justice
orgs.States of Operation: Pennsylvania (Lehigh Valley, Lancaster, Scranton, Reading, York, statewide)
orgs.Evidence Summary: Merger of PA's largest independent power-building orgs (Keystone Progress, Lancaster Stands Up, Beyond the Choir, Reclaim Philadelphia, York Stands Up, Indivisible chapters); Race Class Narrative framework is empirically grounded; STV 2020 partner confirmed in existing sheet notes (ORG-083). Companion c4 (Pennsylvania Stands Up Inc, EIN 83-2880678) handles electoral work.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The only PA organizing group using Race Class Narrative to reach poor white voters in small cities and rural areas -- exactly the voters who swung PA-07, PA-08, and PA-10 toward Republicans -- with chapters already embedded in those districts.
orgs.Key Risks & Flags: (1) Two-entity structure (c3 + c4) requires clarity for donors on which entity to fund and what activities each supports. (2) Financial data not fully verified from ProPublica -- revenue scale unclear without direct 990 review. (3) Existing sheet entry ORG-083 (Pennsylvania Stands Up) already in sheet as a maybe -- this row (Institute/c3) is the companion tax-deductible entity. Confirm deduplication.

orgs.Primary Focus: Runs multiracial community organizing across western Pennsylvania -- Pittsburgh metro, Allegheny County, and surrounding region -- focused on workers, housing, racial justice, and civic engagement including voter registration and GOTV.
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: Civic engagement, Workers, Housing, Racial justice, Voting rights
orgs.Evidence Summary: FY2024 revenue $1.69M confirmed on ProPublica; tax-exempt since 2009 gives 15+ year track record; active in 2025 Pittsburgh Mayor's race as part of WFP IE coalition alongside OnePA, Pennsylvania United PAC, SEIU HCPA, 1Hood, SURJ, Pittsburgh Federation of Teachers.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: Western Pennsylvania's anchor multiracial organizing group -- 15 years of base-building in Pittsburgh, backed by the same WFP-led coalition that turned out voters for Ed Gainey and Sara Innamorato.
orgs.Key Risks & Flags: (1) Western PA geography is less directly tied to tracked 2026 House races (PA-07 is Lehigh Valley, PA-08 is Scranton) -- Pittsburgh is most relevant to Allegheny County state races and the PA-12 congressional seat (Summer Lee, safe D). (2) Revenue at $1.69M is solid but not large -- verify program expense ratio before major gift. (3) 2025 Gainey re-election effort was unsuccessful -- worth understanding post-mortem.

orgs.Primary Focus: Congregation-based community organizing across Philadelphia, the Lehigh Valley, Lancaster, and Central PA -- 50+ congregations representing 30,000+ Pennsylvanians -- running campaigns on economic justice, housing, education funding, climate, and voter registration/GOTV.
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: Civic engagement, Faith communities, Economic justice, Education, Climate, Voting rights
orgs.States of Operation: Pennsylvania (Philadelphia, Lehigh Valley, Lancaster, Central PA -- Bucks, Montgomery, Delaware, Chester counties)
orgs.Evidence Summary: FY2023 revenue $2.98M confirmed per InfluenceWatch; 50+ congregations, 30,000+ members; Democracy Alliance funder; William Penn Foundation, Proteus Fund, and Campaign for Human Development funders confirmed. Founded 2011, 14-year track record including Lehigh Valley POWER-Central chapter (Lancaster, Lebanon, Dauphin, York counties) and POWER-Metro (Bucks, Montgomery, Delaware, Chester).
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The faith-based organizing infrastructure embedded in the exact geographies -- Lehigh Valley, Lancaster, Bucks County -- where PA-07, PA-08, and PA-10 will be decided, with 30,000 congregants as a built-in trusted messenger network.
orgs.Key Risks & Flags: (1) ~15.9% government grant dependency noted in InfluenceWatch -- worth verifying revenue composition and funder concentration. (2) ProPublica entry (EIN 27-4327457) appears to show filings through 2018 -- need to confirm current entity and most recent 990. (3) Faith-based trusted messenger model may be slower to translate to electoral turnout than direct field programs. (4) Budget is largely salary-heavy -- verify program expense ratio.
